description: The agent receives a dataset of real-world municipal vendor payments (`vendor_payments.xlsx`) containing check dates, vendor names, and payment amounts. The agent must perform a forensic audit using Benford's Law by extracting the leading digit of each valid payment (Amount >= $10, no negative/zero), calculating the actual frequency distribution, comparing it against the theoretical Benford distribution ($P(d) = \log_{10}(1 + 1/d)$), and visualizing the results with a Combo Chart (Bar for Actual, Line for Expected). Finally, the agent must identify the digit with the highest positive deviation and extract the top 5 vendors (by total payment sum) associated with that anomalous digit on a new sheet named 'Suspicious_Activity'.
